text: This assumes that everyone reads books because they want a list of actionable steps (that everyone else can also find, with AI or not = you’re replaceable). It’s not about how much information you have. It’s also not about what you do with that information. It’s about changing your mind. Because your mind is how you interact with reality. It’s how you make decisions that lead to a good or bad life. Your mind doesn’t change with a few key points. It changes when new ideas (actionable or not) fill in missing pieces, challenge old beliefs, or give you a new angle to view a situation from. No single person reads a book and gets the exact same lessons out of it. Each individual has their own beliefs, goals, problems, story, and interests that shape how they perceive the information in the book. Let’s say 2 people want to make $1 million. Person A could ignore the key points entirely, still get the right idea that leads to clarity, and make the decisions that lead to $1m. Person B could obsessively study the key points, miss all of the context (what people love to label as “fluff”) and act like a blind puppet without realizing that if everyone had the key points to make $1m (everyone does) nothing would change. Book summaries have been a thing for decades and they don’t do much good because they’re missing exactly what makes those key points work. And last point: The obsession over actionable steps and bullet point summaries is just another manifestation of cheap dopamine and distraction. Read more books. Read longer books. Read books that have nothing to do with what you’re trying to achieve. Read the books that people wouldn’t even think about summarizing with AI. That’s where the gold is. I love AI and where it’s going as much as the next guy, and use it every day, but you’re going to be the one that’s replaced if you can’t do what AI can’t. You aren’t high agency if you let AI make your decisions.

text: I get cluster headaches every 2-3 years. In short, they fucking suck. Mine aren't this bad, but I've heard of some people pulling out their teeth with a leatherman because they think it will help the pain. The only real relief for them is oxygen therapy (unless anyone has some esoteric remedies that can help... I've tried a lot). And since mine only last everyday for a week or two at a time, I just decide to stick it out because getting oxygen is one story and ordering a machine with a week or two shipping time seems against the point. Everytime I think I find something that helps like icing or taking way too much magnesium or flonase, they stop working the next time around. Now this isn't a post to complain. It's the exact opposite. When I'm not rolling around on the floor gripping my head and trying not to pull my hair, life is so amazing. It feels like a privilege to be able to write and work. Food tastes better. The sun feels like I've been stuck inside for 6 months. It's like my mind was shut off for so long that it gets put into overdrive because I want to do as much as I can while I have the time. Maybe there is a lesson here. Maybe it's gratitude. Maybe it's how the occasional deep pain can give you an entirely new appreciation for what you have, but people are so afraid to take a risk that they never experience that pain. I hope you never experience a cluster headache, but I hope you find something to be appreciative of today.
zh: 我每 2-3 年就会出现丛集性头痛。简而言之，他们真他妈糟糕。我的还不错，但我听说有些人退出了……

text: Your future will be determined by 3 things. And no, those things don't involve Uber Eats, TikTok, or standing on the edge because you missed out on Trump coin (guilty). No exceptions. 3) The ability to persist and iterate. You don't quit. You pivot. There is a series of actions that lead to the life you want. You will have to change direction multiple times. If your actions create your future, and you aren't where you want to be, then wave goodbye to almost everything you do on a daily basis.

text: Closing in on the big 1m and pretty annoyed I go through these lulls in views every few months. Videos do really well, then 4-5 of them do way worse. When I look back on them, I realize that the videos just aren't good enough. Not that the content itself is bad or not helpful, but you can see a drastic difference in the topic, title, and thumbnail choice. I can almost always map these "lulls" to a period when ideas aren't flowing well. I'm not reading as much. I'm not learning anything new. I'm trying to force things out for the sake of staying consistent. What's helped me break through these points is just that: Doing a mental reset. Throwing all planned content ideas out the window and starting from scratch. Picking up a new book. Learning from new people. Realigning with the core ideas that I'm best at articulating and adding a twist. The reason I write this is because I see many others go through the same lulls, or they never make it out of the beginner lull that everyone starts out in. What helps is just being brutally honest with how good the video actually is. It can be good in your eyes, which is important, but if others don't care about it, it's missing a crucial aspect of what makes it "good." (P.S. I know 50k+ views is nothing to scoff at. Speaking relative to the normal baseline.)

text: If I was starting from zero, here's what I would do: 1) Start on social media. I know I know. It's cringe and overblown at this point. But you have to understand a few points: - social media is the new media - media is where the attention is - media is how you attract people to your independent work - anyone can become a one-person media company - it's free, accessible, and arguably the highest leverage form of media today Whether you like it or not, if you want to do your own thing, social media is your best option. And no, it's not even close to saturated. Once you actually have experience in it, you see it. Last point: Building on social media forces you to *learn skills that actually matter.* You don't have to guess whether or not they are "worth learning" because you see the results. 2) Pair your personal interests with media-based skills. Start by attracting people to your interests. - Write about them while studying persuasion (posts, threads, newsletters) - Learn design basics for your banner, profile picture, and images - Supplement with education on audience building, storytelling, attention capture, etc. You learn faster when you have a project to apply your learning to. The cool thing about this: *You can apply almost any skill or interest you learn to social media.* Don't underestimate that power. 3) Monetize as soon as possible. You can wait to monetize an audience, or you can start right away. I recommend the latter. Why? Because the faster you fail, the faster you succeed. And, if you want it to do well, you need to again supplement with relative skills like offer creation. Your first product/service will suck whether you do it now or wait 6 months. - look at relevant products you've purchased in the past - look at who you follow and what they sell - think about the most impactful books you've read - look in your google drive or Kortex to see what you can slap a price tag on - create something similar, but with your own slight twist Business 101: sell what's already selling, but slightly better. Then, get unique and innovative with your next product if you want. If you want to learn how to create a micro-product or service, read this previous letter: thedankoe.com/letters/the-si…
zh: 如果我从零开始，我会这样做：1）从社交媒体开始。我知道我知道。这是令人畏缩和过分夸大的…

text: It doesn't matter how long it takes. You started something new because you want a sense of zest in your life. The default mindless way of life wasn't cutting it for you. By quitting, you are effectively telling yourself that you aren't worth it. 4 years of school is fine. But 6-12 months building your own thing is too much? Because you have better things you could be doing? Really? What are those "better things?" More time to lounge around in front of a screen? Gain some fucking perspective. There is absolutely nothing more important than avoiding a robotic life.
zh: 需要多长时间并不重要。你开始了一些新的事情，因为你想要生活充满热情。默认无意识...

text: Writing is more than communication. Writing is how you practice thinking. Writing is how you document your development. Writing is how you attract supporters for your work. Writing will only go out of style when you outsource the creative ability of your mind.

text: You need 4 activities: Consume: learn something new. Create: build a meaningful project. Connect: with people and nature. Clear: your mind of rogue thoughts. Most of your problems stem from a stagnant mind that can be solved with a structure that encourages flow.

text: Everyone should write a book at least once in their lifetime. Especially if you want to become a deep and articulate thinker. When you write a post or article, your mind expands to fit that context. You catch ideas that help you finish that specific project. But a book is different. It's extremely chaotic at first. You spill your subconscious into a blank document and start shaping it into chapters and sections. You create a little world for your mind to operate in. The longer you spend in that world, the more your mind expands to take that shape, and the more connections you start to make. When you're writing a book, you struggle a lot, but you'll find that your brain lights up with interest and excitement more than ever before. Almost any information or conversation you come across gives you an idea. You start to realize that your mind is only as perceptive as the project you're working on.
zh: 每个人一生中至少应该写一本书。特别是如果你想成为一名深刻而善于表达的思想家。当你...

text: It's scary to admit that everything you believe is a story you've accepted or invented.

text: You tried to fit in. You tried to follow the crowd. You tried to do the things that people told you would lead to success. But you're different. You're observant. You just lack clarity on how to create your own path. This may help: youtu.be/cG-brqeMrBw

text: The easiest way to figure out what you want in life is to make a habit out of reflecting on what you don't want. Then, all you have to do is master the art of saying no to that. Most people are stuck in their stupid busy lives. They don't register what they don't want because it's normal to them at this point. You need to sit down. Reflect on your month without getting distracted. Journal it on paper if you have to. It's incredibly difficult to change if you aren't aware of what to change.

text: Balance is found in the extremes. Pushing to failure. Prioritizing recovery. Deep focused work. Worry-free rest. Periods of intensity for rapid and rare progress followed by periods of consistency to maintain new baselines. Stuck in the middle is a slow and average death.

text: Stop giving attention to things you don't want more of in your life. Negative thoughts. Behavior of other people. Anything you read. Anything you watch. text: You only encounter mistakes if you're actually doing something. For most people, they see this as a bad thing. text: "But how do I start it?" You tell me. Open up the video editor, record a video without overthinking it, start editing, and I guarantee you will find mistakes that you can start improving much faster than if you spent 10 hours studying and not building. Open a document and braindump some ideas. Write a post and post it even if it's terrible. Just start doing something. Anything. If you aren't moving you are decaying. If you don't, you're simply delaying the amount of time of getting your first terrible iteration out. Doesn't matter if you spend 5 minutes or 10 years on it. The. First. Iteration. Will. Suck. Hitting "send" is nature's filter for people who aren't serious about making it.

text: One of the most important skills is how to learn skills fast. You need to be able to adapt going into a future of AI and automation. And learning one skill will only lead to you being replaced. But most people don't know how to learn. 1) Stop Learning "Skills" The first problem is that people think they learn skills by picking one and studying it. No. That's how you learn the same thing as everyone else and get placed in a job or business that can be replaced in a millisecond. Instead, you learn anything and everything that allows you to achieve your ideal lifestyle. Everything must be filtered through your vision. 2) Start The Project, Then Learn Most people get trapped in tutorial hell. They watch videos all day and after 3 months they still have no idea what they're doing when they decide to start. Start a project that will move the needle toward your vision. Start. It. Then, when you hit a roadblock, supplement with education. 3) Ship The Project Post the video. Send the newsletter. Launch the product. Pitch your service. Especially if you're embarrassed. The harsh reality is that if you don't have real people using your project, you have no idea if it's valuable. You don't determine its value. If you never hit send, you have no idea if you're moving in the right direction, and you will get lost. I'll end with this: You can't improve what doesn't exist, therefore you don't actually start learning a thing until you put your project in front of people to judge, criticize, and use.
zh: 最重要的技能之一就是如何快速学习技能。您需要能够适应...

text: Yeah they're decently comparable right now. We don't have databases (or care to) or other dashboard style features. Atm the differentiation is the capture system, pane layouts for writers, connections, and tags. Gamechangers for some, but Notion users may not care for them until they test their workflow out in it. In 4-6 months, I don't think they'll be comparable aside from document editing, like most editors or note-takers are. Not in the sense that we'll be better than Notion, that'll be tough haha. But in the sense that we will serve a different use case entirely and it will be complimentary to Notion if people want to do project management and stuff in there. We're heading more in the direction of capturing anything seamlessly, organizing it without wasting time, and surfacing unique and relevant ideas right when you need them for your writing, content, outlines, projects, etc. I will say, I do think our team will surprise a lot of people in that timeframe.

text: Why doesn't finding the "best" niche work? Well... just look at how many people don't quit, but there are other reasons. 1) By default, you're entering a space that you know nothing about with people you don't care about. 2) That niche isn't going to be profitable if you don't understand it inside and out. 3) You are already part of a niche. You already have skill and expertise that's helped you... so it can help someone else. So, stop trying to "find" a niche. Create a niche by attracting the people you can help the most. Another problem: When you focus on a niche, you box yourself in for no reason and limit yourself. When you focus on pain points, you resonate with anyone with those pain points. In other words, you gain more leverage and reach without sacrificing the benefits of niching down. The lesson: Focus on one burning pain point. (This answers the "why should they care" question) Create a product or service that resolves it. (This answers the "what can I help them achieve" question). Write content around that pain point to attract people that you can help the most. If you can persist through trial and error, I have no doubt you'll win. --- Ad (scroll away if you don't like ads): By the way, the One-Person Business Launchpad is 50% off until the end of Black Friday weekend. >> thedankoe.com/get/opbl Grab it here if you want arguably the simplest way to start a modern business.

text: A little known truth: Skills are groups of techniques. What?? When you start trying to acquire a skill like Photoshop, you don't sit around studying the software all day. Instead, you start creating the project. Then you hit a roadblock. You don't know how to complete a certain part of it. So, you go on YouTube and look up how to do *that specific thing.* As you repeat this to build the project, you may not understand all of Photoshop, but you have a group of techniques that allow you to do quite a lot with the software. In something like Photoshop, you really only need to know a few things: selections, masks, and adjustment layers. Once you know those, you can create practically anything. The same holds true with every single skill imaginable. When you're building a business or side hustle, you don't acquire the skill of "business." You don't even acquire the skill of "marketing." You have a goal: land a customer. You study a technique: how to write an engaging social post. You study another: how to write a short promotion. You study another: how to build a simple landing page. You study another: how to accept payment. When you combine all of those, you achieve Level 1 of making money on your own. The lesson: Build more projects. Learn more techniques. Notice if you're getting closer to your goal. Try, fail, and persist until you achieve the goal, then repeat. --- After 4 years, I'm finally listening. The most common question I get is, "Okay, I like the concept of a one-person business, but how do I *actually* start one." Almost nobody is teaching the absolute beginner steps to get up and running to make your first $5K (when you have no idea where to start or what to sell). So, The One-Person Business Launchpad is 50% off through Black Friday weekend. text: "Educated" isn't the right word. "Schooled" or "indoctrinated" is. This isn't a fight between red and blue. This is a fight between freedom and slavery. Slavery of the mind. The difference between schooling and education is the difference between imposed order and emergent order. Schooling offers a narrow and rigid path that serves the dominant social system. If you don't conform to that path by memorizing facts and regurgitating information, you are punished. Bad grades and the threat of not getting a job. Schooling makes you a useful worker. If you understand how the mind works, you understand how large of a trap it is to narrow your mind on one singular path – that you did not willfully choose, but were conditioned to pursue – because it leads to stupidity and dependency on someone else for your survival. Think about it. You are enticed into going to school because it's the only path you know. You only know that one path because your parents and teachers also went through their own schooling. The teachers learned how to become teachers through other teachers. Your parents survival is probably dependent on the specialist work they were trained into by a system of teachers teaching teachers how to become teachers. You want a degree because of status. You want the title because you think it will help you survive. So your mind orients itself with that goal and everything else becomes unimportant. Over the course of 4-12 years, you do almost nothing aside from study a biased curriculum and relieve stress through pleasure when you can. Your mind becomes hardened and reactive by default. Education, on the other hand, offers no path. Education is discovery. Education is trial and error toward a goal that you deem meaningful. Education is the process of acquiring the knowledge and skill necessary to create a meaningful life. Education comes down to two main topics: 1) Learning how to learn. 2) Learning how to live. Neither of which are taught in the school system. You are told what to learn and how to live rather than discovering it for yourself. As humans, we derive a lot of meaning from how we contribute to the world. Deep down, we all want to do work that matters. We all want to contribute in a meaningful way to our tribe and world. If our work is imposed on us, or assigned to us, it's no wonder why we can't find an inherent meaning to our life. That alone explains much of the vocal "blue" behavior. The only meaningful and important thing to them is making sure that others fall in line and conform to the same standards they did because they failed to think for themselves. There are 2 options here. 1) Reform the education system. 2) Pursue your own interest-based education. The first can be influenced by exposing the current systems flaws. It's going to take work, waking people up, and new solutions being built by independent thinkers. The second can happen at any time. You simply have to realize that floating through life isn't going to take you anywhere meaningful. In fact, doing nothing by your own desire only leads to people taking advantage of you. Question everything. Contemplate your place on this planet. Think deeply about what you want out of life. Pursue the interests that will take you there. You have access to the internet. If you have the intense desire to learn, build, and adapt, you'll turn out just fine.
zh: “受过教育”这个词不合适。 “学校”或“灌输”是。这不是红蓝之战。这是自由与……

As of now, for most of the note taking crowd, it's not worth the effort to switch unless you want something minimal and purely focused on capturing and organizing ideas. text: 7/ How to write better than 99% of people: 1) Pick a topic you love to read about 2) Search for an idea you want to make your own Then: Use the Problem & Process framework. Start with a pain point people deal with as the hook. > A pain point captures attention and qualifies the reader. Then, list of steps for them to solve that pain point. > Digestible steps hold attention and deliver value. You can use the Problem & Process framework for: - A simple social media post - A newsletter outline - A thread outline For the last two, start with the pain, then list out key points in your actionable steps and explain them in more depth than you would a social post. Most successful people will tell you that writing is a non-negotiable task in their day. So, if you want to make your work a success: Write. Daily. Attract an audience. Pitch your expertise to that audience. If something isn't working, it's because you aren't skilled enough yet.

text: It's okay to get the job because you need money. It's okay to start a business because you want more. It's okay to make as much as possible, but if you continue to ignore your unconscious impact on humanity, you'll wonder why your bank is so full, yet your soul is so empty.

text: For those wondering what's going on in the vid: - The little toggles inside the documents are called Elements. You can build templates, swipe files, or outlines with them. You can also think of them as atomic notes you can search for, open in the viewport, and link to any other note Examples: I use them to write all of my posts in so I can find any post fast. I also break my newsletters / youtube scripts into elements so each section of them becomes a note I can find at any time (good for writers that don't care to take notes, but understand the importance of referencing past ideas) - The little purple links are connections and tags. If you've used a second brain before you know what these are. They help you never lose an idea. - You can open any document, connection, tab (like the library), or element in a pane on the right hand side to reference while you write. - The library allows you to connect with Readwise (for now) so you can save web clips, tweets, kindle highlights, and more. You can link these in documents or search for them pretty fast while writing. - The capture feature didn't get much love at the end, but think of it like texting ideas to yourself. But those ideas can be tagged and connected to documents. Example: I'm writing a second book and my documents are organized by chapter. Whenever I have an idea for a specific chapter, I can capture an idea to that specific chapter and have all ideas open while I'm writing (screenshot attached) P.S. If you have a readwise account and bookmark this tweet, it will be added to your Kortex when you sign up :)

text: If you feel lost, write down: 1) Who you want to become 2) Who you don't want to become 3) The 3-5 things you can do today You're lost because you don't have a direction to work toward, an enemy to work away from, and tasks that move the needle toward your goals.

text: Your ideas get better with layers of execution. Putting an idea on paper is layer 1. Designing how it will work is layer 2. Building it is layer 3. Testing it is layer 4. User feedback is layer 5. Iteration is layer 6. Then, repeating the process and ending up with ideas that were impossible for you to initially come up with continues to compound into something truly unique. The lesson: You have absolutely zero idea what your potential is until you finally execute on the ideas that are currently in your head. Waiting for the "best" idea is stupid and impossible.
zh: 通过层层执行，您的想法会变得更好。将想法写在纸上是第 1 层。设计它的工作方式是第 2 层……

text: Execution matters. A lot. But when anyone can code, write, design, and build with new technology and AI, the quality, novelty, and usefulness of your ideas matter more. Ideas over execution. Execution over doing nothing because you think the world is ending.

text: If you want to enjoy the journey, choose the right destination.
zh: 如果您想享受这段旅程，请选择正确的目的地。

text: My potentially unpopular beliefs: - Destination > journey - Everyone should start a business - Selfishness comes before selflessness - Spirituality is discovered in superficial mistakes - You are never standing still or "chilling," you are dying List yours.

text: You aren't afraid of failure, you're afraid of what people will think of you if you fail. The thing is, nobody is watching and nobody cares. And even if they do, they'll get distracted and forget about it within an hour or two.
zh: 你并不害怕失败，你害怕的是如果你失败了人们会怎么看你。问题是，没有人在看……

text: How to do whatever you want: Do whatever you want, but when you make a mistake, don't brush it under the rug and get trapped in a loop of pleasure and pain. Fix the mistake, reassess what you actually want, and repeat the process.
zh: 如何做你想做的事：做你想做的事，但当你犯了错误时，不要把它掩盖起来并得到……

text: Last thing, those who are confident in how long they work don't need to be told otherwise. If you aren't confident in how you are working and its results, try it instead of holding onto a belief that clearly isn't serving you. Short, intentional, focused work throughout the day
zh: 最后一件事，那些对自己工作时间有信心的人不需要被告知。如果您对如何...

text: A rule of thumb: **If you can't make substantial progress in 2-4 hours, you have no business working 12.** At that point you are wasting crucial time and frankly don't know what you're doing. Save yourself the pain, refocus, and don't let other areas of your life go to shit.
zh: 经验法则： **如果您无法在 2-4 小时内取得实质性进展，那么您就没有工作 12.** 那时您...

text: you aren't wired for 8 hour workdays
zh: 您每天工作 8 小时都没有连接

text: Most of your problems in today's world are resource management problems. You have access to any information you need to do whatever you want. You can't focus for more than 30 seconds. You get distracted and quit after 2 weeks. You have the resources. You don't have the clarity.
zh: 当今世界的大多数问题都是资源管理问题。你可以访问任何你需要的信息来做任何你想做的事。...

text: The matrix is holding you down. The elites are in your house forcing you to eat, scroll, and stay inside. You have to go to college and take out loans. Books and radios are still the only way to learn. Nobody is building a decent and successful life. In this economy? Impossible.
zh: 矩阵正在压制你。精英们就在你的房子里，迫使你吃饭、滚动、呆在家里。你必须走了……

text: It's such a blessing to be on social media. Tons of successful people passing down what worked for them available for you to steal, test, and experiment with. Maybe I need to have more empathy but it's difficult for me to understand how people don't see results when the answer is right there. It could be about getting ripped, it could be about building a business, it could be about meeting new people. It could be free. It could be behind a paywall. It could be anywhere but it's there. Yes, everyone's situation is unique and sometimes the problem is much deeper... but what you don't see is that too is a problem that can be solved. You aren't helpless. It's not like the successful people are spewing random advice out of their ass. Some are, sure, but you could easily filter what's useful from what's not by just doing it and making a mistake. You probably learn more that way anyway. Atleast by making the mistake you gain a bit more clarity on what to do. Nature's compass. Trial and error. That's the problem. You don't DO. You don't test. You don't experiment. And if you do, you stop after making a mistake or two, but that mistake is your golden ticket. That's the small light in the unknown that shows you where not to go, and if you stack enough of those failures the entire room lights up and you know exactly where to go. So, that makes me think that people just read and read and read and mentally masturbate to the info. "Ah fuck yeah another golden startup tip" "Ah niceee another one" "Ah yes another one for the collection let me do absolutely nothing with it. I feel good about myself because I acquired a little idea that will dissipate by tomorrow." Your mind doesn't say that but your actions do. You don't have a project you are building to test the information on. You haven't sat down and promised yourself that you are going to commit to something and make it work. You can't navigate your own mind because your compulsive negative thoughts are in the way making you belive it isn't possible when it clearly is and has been done before. If one person has done it, it's possible. Even if it's not, it's foolish to believe it's not, because a bit of failure is a lot more fun than a metric ton of mediocrity. Please just take the next idea you see – that aligns with a goal *you chose* to pursue – put your phone down, and test it.
zh: 出现在社交媒体上真是一件幸事。大量成功人士传承了对他们有用的东西，可供你窃取，……

text: solve your own problems and pass down the solution
zh: 解决自己的问题，并把解决办法传下去

text: If you've lived the same day over and over, you are either very miserable or very fulfilled. The difference is that one is assigned a life they never question and the other goes through the pain to create their own.
zh: 如果你一遍又一遍地过着同一天，你要么很痛苦，要么很充实。不同之处在于，一个人被分配了……

text: Being a creator is being an investigative journalist of your own mind.
zh: 作为一名创作者，就是成为一名有自己想法的调查记者。

text: One single idea can change the direction of your life. You're most likely to find that idea in a book. One that you chose to read out of curiosity, not because your teacher assigned it to you so you could pass a class. More reasons to read: youtu.be/jNUFWPqz3Wk
zh: 一个想法可以改变你的生活方向。您很可能在一本书中找到这个想法。您选择的一个...

text: The harsh truth is that you probably outsourced your certainty and never took it back. Parents, teachers, and authorities programmed your mind to work towards the outdated goals of their generation. Now you're stuck wondering what to do with your future: thedankoe.com/letters/the-un…
zh: 严酷的事实是您可能将确定性外包出去并且再也没有收回。父母、老师和当局将你的思维编程为工作……

text: Most people don’t understand the bliss of being locked in. They say, “Why don’t you take a break and have some fun?” They can’t see that nothing else is more fun than what you are doing.
zh: 大多数人不理解被锁在里面的幸福。他们说，“你为什么不休息一下，找点乐子呢？”他们不能……

text: A big reason people never change is because they never get absolutely fed up with the current version of themselves. Not uncomfortable enough to change, just comfortable enough to stay the same.
zh: 人们从不改变的一个重要原因是他们永远不会完全厌倦自己当前的版本。还不够难受...

text: The difference between the successful and unsuccessful is attention and value. The unsuccessful fail to bring attention to themselves and their work, so they lack feedback to make that work valuable. You can work all day long, but if nobody sees it or cares about it, you lose.
zh: 成功和不成功的区别在于关注度和价值。不成功的人未能引起人们对自己和他们的工作的关注，因此他们...

text: There will be compensation depending on: 1) If you actually like the app after testing it and would use it whether you were being paid or not. 2) Your audience size. We want to work with many smaller creators, but there's an obvious difference in big vs small audience.
zh: 补偿取决于： 1) 如果您在测试后确实喜欢该应用程序，并且无论您是...

text: We need your help. We're looking for your favorite creators who talk about: - Writing - Productivity - Social media - Knowledge management That you could see working with @kortexco as we near public launch. Small, medium, large, doesn't matter. Tag them below.
zh: 我们需要您的帮助。我们正在寻找您最喜欢的创作者，他们谈论以下内容： - 写作 - 生产力 - 社交媒体 - 知识管理...

text: The easier you try to make your life the harder it's going to be. You avoid taking risks and constant improvement without realizing that when you try to stay the same, you don't stay the same. You slowly drown in problems until it's 10 times harder to get out.
zh: 你越想让自己的生活变得轻松，它就会变得越困难。你避免冒险和不断改进，却没有意识到……

text: Every human grows into a unique set of problems. As you solve them, you become unique and valuable. The problem is that most people attach to the rules and goals that society assigns them. Their problems sit around compounding into chaos, and they wonder why they feel so lost.
zh: 每个人都会遇到一系列独特的问题。当你解决这些问题时，你就会变得独特且有价值。问题是大多数人...

text: If you're lost, start solving problems. The problems that are staring you straight in the face. Your fitness, health, finances, mindset, work, or anything preventing you from having control over your life. Experiment until you become obsessed. That's how you find purpose.
zh: 如果你迷路了，就开始解决问题。那些直面你的问题。你的健身、健康、财务、心态、工作或任何东西......

text: There are many habits needed to do this, but reading is a very potent one: thedankoe.com/letters/readin…
zh: 要做到这一点需要很多习惯，但阅读是一个非常有效的习惯：thedankoe.com/letters/readin......

text: You're distracted because you don't have something you find so important that you can't help but focus on it.
zh: 你分心是因为你没有发现如此重要的事情，以至于你无法不去关注它。

text: Build a life you don't feel the need to distract yourself from.
zh: 建立一种足够投入的生活，让自己不再需要持续用干扰逃离它。

text: If your work doesn't allow for evolution, iteration, and creativity - pay attention to that: thedankoe.com/letters/the-fu…
zh: 如果你的工作不允许进化、迭代和创造力 - 请注意：thedankoe.com/letters/the-fu…

text: Burn the word "iteration" into your brain. Nothing worth doing will be perfect the first time around. It will suck. Bad. You can't have a genius idea without a good one, and a good idea without a bad one. Become an idea to execution machine and watch yourself get "lucky."
zh: 将“迭代”这个词铭刻在你的大脑中。没有什么值得做的事情第一次就完美。它会很糟糕。坏的。你不能拥有……

text: The difference between a mind and a supercomputer is that the mind can program itself.
zh: 思维和超级计算机之间的区别在于思维可以自行编程。

text: Reading is how you acquire new knowledge. Reading is how you expand your worldview. Reading is how you spot new opportunities. Reading is how you absorb 10 years of experience in 8 hours. When you're bored, pick up a book, not your phone. Read: thedankoe.com/letters/readin…
zh: 阅读是获取新知识的方式。读书是拓展世界观的途径。阅读是你发现新机会的方式。阅读就是……

text: If you want someone to change, don’t try to change them.
zh: 如果你想让某人改变，就不要试图改变他们。

text: The answer to most of your questions is “try it.”
zh: 许多问题无法靠继续思考解决，真正有用的答案是亲自尝试。

text: It's all just solving problems.
zh: 都是解决问题而已。

text: Action is seamless when clarity is extreme. Get clear on what you want. Get clear on what you don't want. Reverse engineer them into clear goals. Yearly, monthly, weekly, daily. Once you have clarity, you can forget about the goals and focus on building, failing, and learning.
zh: 当清晰度极高时，动作是无缝的。明确你想要什么。明确你不想要什么。对它们进行逆向工程...

text: The reason most don't change is because they don't have clarity on how to change. If you think of your life as a game, it becomes a lot easier: thedankoe.com/letters/the-fl…
zh: 大多数人不改变的原因是因为他们不清楚如何改变。如果你把你的生活当作一场游戏，...

text: You don't want to change because you haven't truly realized where your life will end up if you stay in the same situation. At some point, the pain will outweigh the pleasure. You'll either have to use the pain as energy to change or get trapped wondering where it all went wrong.
zh: 你不想改变，因为你还没有真正意识到，如果你停留在同样的情况下，你的生活将会走向何方。在…

text: Your writing isn't interesting because you don't understand "interest." You aren't born interested in something. How you were raised, your programming, the problems you are going through, and your goals all determine what captures your attention and is seen as valuable.
zh: 有趣的写作，来自理解什么塑造了你的注意力、问题、目标和价值判断。

text: Most people will think you’re inauthentic (because they don’t know what authenticity is - a lack of conformity) but you can decide to be a completely new person in an instant. You can decide to stop being who others programmed you to be and start becoming who you want to be.
zh: 真实并不是保留旧身份，而更多是拒绝无意识地从众。
